sources_exist.note: Six distinct URLs were opened for seven canonical citation occurrences. The Dan Cumberland Labs historical-analog URL returned an internal error and no cited article content; all other URLs resolved to substantive pages.

figures_match_sources.note: The ROG page supports A/E-study figures of 257 transactions, 3.35x lower quartile, 5.30x median, and 5.91x mean, while Design1st supports three billing models and a $100-$450 hourly range. However, the cited pages do not establish industrial-design buy/exit multiples, the asserted 55/35/10 revenue mix, 52.3% of employer businesses owned by people 55+, 2.34x faster diffusion, or zero current PE-backed consolidators. Autodesk is a global cross-industry survey and does not establish 30% material adoption in NAICS 541420.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Declared values are bounded and rationales are concrete, but the role judgment relabels the deterministic 27-1025 Interior Designers occupation as industrial/product designers even though the prompt requires within-role judgments using the supplied role mix. That changes a 0.1841 wage-share contribution on an unsupported occupational premise. t50=4.0 also depends on the inaccessible 2.34x analog. LOW_CONFIDENCE and the three ESTIMATE inputs are appropriately disclosed but do not cure the score-affecting evidence gaps.

rubric_consistent.note: Dataset inputs match the supplied deterministic dataset exactly and ranges/terminal class follow the frozen rubric, but M and B rely on unsupported NAICS-specific fallbacks: 3.50x buy, 5.50x exit, 30% owners 60+, and zero active consolidators. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is not itself disqualifying because the orchestrator separately checks arithmetic, but the underlying researched inputs remain unresolved.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four mandatory cross-checks are answered candidly. The record discloses the labor-share artifact, distinguishes PRESSURED demand from cost cutting, confirms no deal proxy in V/A, and explicitly carries hourly/fixed-fee deflation into pi_dist and pi_moat.

source_audits.note: The 2023 article identifies historic strategic acquisitions including McKinsey/Lunar, Accenture/Fjord, and frog's owners and characterizes design acquisitions as human-capital/capability deals. It does not survey 2024-2025 PE-backed platforms and cannot establish the score-affecting count of zero active consolidators.

ROG reports 257 architecture, engineering, and environmental-consulting transactions and a 3.35x all-firms lower-quartile EV/EBITDA multiple. It does not cover NAICS 541420 industrial-design firms, identify small/single-location deal size, support the additional 3.0x-5.0x consulting range, or establish the selected 3.50x buy multiple.

Autodesk describes 5,399 respondents across 18 countries and multiple design-and-make sectors and says more than half were approaching or had achieved an AI-incorporation goal. It does not measure material AI use by US NAICS 541420 firms, support the separate 54% weekly-designer-use assertion, or establish the selected 30% adoption input.

The page supports 5.30x median and 5.91x mean EV/EBITDA for its mixed A/E transaction database. It neither isolates $10M+ EBITDA platforms nor industrial-design transactions, so it does not establish a 5.50x NAICS 541420 platform exit multiple.

Opening the exact cited URL produced an internal error with no accessible article content. Searches did not surface the claimed 2.34x diffusion figure at that URL; the citation therefore cannot support the analog or the derived four-year t50.

The Project Equity page says 2.9 million US businesses are owned by people aged 55+ and presents general exit-planning statistics. It does not state that 52.3% of employer businesses are owned by people 55+, does not measure age 60+, and provides no industrial-design-specific basis for the selected 30%.

Design1st supports flat fee, fee-for-service, and reduced-fee-plus-equity/royalty models and quotes $100-$450 hourly rates. It provides no revenue-share survey, retainer share, or 12-24 month rebid cycle, so it does not establish the score-affecting 55/35/10 billing mix used in pi_dist.

reasons: At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://dancumberlandlabs.com/blog/ai-adoption-curve/), the exact source is inaccessible and no cited content establishes the 2.34x diffusion figure used to derive inputs.t50_years.value=4.0. Re-run with an accessible primary or credible industry source that documents the analog, population, and timing chain.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://project-equity.org/news/employee-ownership-insider/business-owner-statistics-exit-planning/), the page does not contain the quoted 52.3% employer-business statistic and does not measure owners age 60+ in NAICS 541420. Re-run with an auditable age-60-plus industry estimate and explicit derivation from the cited population.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.rog-partners.com/inside-the-latest-a/e-business-valuation-and-m-a-transaction-study), the source is a mixed A/E database with no industrial-design, small-target, or $10M+ platform segmentation. Re-run with transaction evidence matched to the required industry and size scopes, or a fully documented comparable-industry normalization chain.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://design1st.com/how-product-design-firms-charge/), the source names pricing models but does not support the asserted 55% fixed-fee, 35% hourly, 10% retainer mix or rebid timing that drives inputs.pi_dist. Re-run with sourced revenue shares or an explicit, independently supported estimate chain.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[0], the record reinterprets supplied occupation 27-1025 Interior Designers as industrial/product designers and assigns its 0.1841 wage share a 0.40 automatable fraction. Re-run using the supplied occupation as identified, or provide an auditable crosswalk/alternate role dataset authorized by the prompt before applying industrial-designer tasks.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://medium.com/@teaque.lenahan/how-to-consume-a-frog-opportunities-and-challenges-of-integrating-design-firms-into-global-742a88f71043), a 2023 narrative about historic strategic acquisitions cannot establish zero PE-backed add-on platforms in the last 24 months. Re-run with a documented current buyer search or transaction database evidence supporting the count.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://adsknews.autodesk.com/en/pressrelease/2024-state-of-design-and-make-report-from-autodesk-finds-growing-confidence-in-resilience-optimism-toward-ai/), a global, multi-sector sentiment survey does not establish 30% material AI adoption among US NAICS 541420 firms and does not support the separately quoted 54% weekly-use figure. Re-run with a source and adjustment chain matched to material firm-level use and the target population.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (buy_mult, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S
